query: We surveyed 80 users. 28% say they would be 'very disappointed' if they could no longer use our product.
expected_behavior: Identifies this as below the Sean Ellis 40% threshold — a PMF signal requiring action
Does not celebrate the 28% — frames it as a pivot signal
Applies the PMF Diagnostics framework: which assumption is wrong? Product, market, or channel?
Produces a structured pivot recommendation rather than 'just keep improving the product'
Does not recommend expanding the feature set as the first response to a sub-40% PMF score
